seoTitle: How to Export Your Full Webflow Website Code
metaDescription: Extract and package your entire Webflow project's front-end source code for independent hosting or full source ownership.
intro: Sometimes you need complete ownership of your site's source files, whether for archival purposes or moving to a fully self-hosted setup. This guide covers using Webflow's Code Export panel to generate a clean zip of your HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and image assets. It's for developers and agencies who want a full local copy of a project's front-end code.

steps.heading: Open the Code Export panel
steps.body: In the Webflow Designer, find the Export Code shortcut in the top toolbar to open the extraction console.

steps.heading: Generate the code package
steps.body: Trigger the export process to compile your HTML, CSS, and JavaScript into a downloadable static build.

steps.heading: Review the folder structure
steps.body: Check the exported directory to confirm your pages, media assets, and script files all mapped over cleanly.

steps.heading: Download the zip file
steps.body: Save the complete static website package locally, then extract it to inspect the raw HTML and stylesheet files.

steps.heading: Fix broken paths and forms
steps.body: Check for broken image links after extraction, and set up an external form handling endpoint since native Webflow forms won't work outside the platform.

faq.question: Is code export available on all Webflow plans?
faq.answer: No, exporting code typically requires a paid site plan, so verify your plan's features before relying on this workflow.

faq.question: What happens to my forms after exporting?
faq.answer: Native Webflow form handling doesn't carry over, so you'll need to connect an external service to process form submissions on your new host.

faq.question: Can I re-edit the exported code visually in Webflow later?
faq.answer: No, the export is a static snapshot; any further visual editing needs to happen back in the original Webflow project and be re-exported.

faq.question: Will exported code work offline?
faq.answer: Mostly yes, though any features relying on live Webflow services like forms and CMS data will need external replacements.
